skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit AI images through RunComfy using model-specific routing for text-to-image and image-to-image tasks.
skill.summary: Route image generation and editing requests to suitable RunComfy models.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns, but most are Markdown examples or model documentation. Confirmed risks are legitimate for this skill: it runs the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and image references to RunComfy services, writes output files, and documents token storage.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Executes External Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to invoke runcomfy commands and allows Bash(runcomfy *). This is expected, but users should trust the installed CLI and review generated inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is explicit in allowed-tools and command examples. It is legitimate for the skill, but it remains a real execution boundary.

medium_findings.title: Cloud API Calls and Generated File Downloads
medium_findings.description: The CLI posts prompts and image references to RunComfy, polls job status, and downloads generated files. This requires user awareness of outbound data flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly describes API posting, polling, and output downloads. The endpoints are vendor-owned, so this is expected network behavior.

medium_findings.title: Credential Storage Requires User Care
medium_findings.description: The skill documents token storage at a local config path and supports RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. Tokens must not be logged, prompted, or checked into repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and environment variable are explicitly documented. The file mode guidance lowers risk, but credentials remain sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image References Can Influence Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts reference image URLs and optional web grounding. These inputs can steer model output through embedded text or external context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill itself warns that reference URLs and web search results are untrusted. This is a semantic risk for generated output integrity.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command hits are Markdown code spans, model identifiers, or fenced Bash examples. No Ruby code or backtick shell execution was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose, tables, or examples. There is no executable Ruby source in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are URL Placeholders
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts come from placeholder URLs containing ellipses. They are not local filesystem paths or traversal operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations show https://... examples inside JSON input snippets. They do not resolve or access local paths.

low_findings.title: Command Substitution Pattern Is Discussed as Safe Input Text
low_findings.description: The $(...) pattern appears in a security note explaining that prompt text is transmitted as JSON and not shell-expanded by the CLI.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is quoted in explanatory documentation, not executed. The surrounding text is a mitigation note about shell injection.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Keywords Not Found
low_findings.description: No evidence found of instructions telling evaluators to ignore prior instructions, override the audit, or treat the skill as pre-approved.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted search for common prompt-injection phrases found no matches. The skill discusses indirect prompt injection only as a user mitigation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed External CLI Boundary
dangerous_patterns.description: The manifest allows Bash(runcomfy *), so the marketplace should present this as a command-running skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is direct evidence. The scope is limited to runcomfy commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Model API Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow sends prompts and image references to RunComfy services and downloads output artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The How it works and Security sections explicitly describe outbound API calls and downloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Token File
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI stores a token in a user config file after login. This is normal for CLI authentication but sensitive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permissions are explicitly named. The documented 0600 mode is an important mitigation.

content.value_statement: Choosing the right image model is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill routes prompts to RunComfy models and supplies practical generation settings.
actual_capabilities: Routes text-to-image requests across RunComfy model families.
Routes image-to-image edits to models suited for preservation or text changes.
Provides model-specific prompt patterns and parameter guidance.
Uses the runcomfy CLI with output directory support.
Documents authentication, token handling, exit codes, and network boundaries.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Sends prompts and image references to RunComfy cloud services.
Does not provide mask-driven inpainting workflows directly.
Model availability, pricing, and output quality can change outside the skill.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate posters, product images, and multilingual creative assets with model choices matched to layout or text needs.

use_cases.title: Explore Concept Directions
use_cases.description: Produce fast moodboards, storyboard frames, and visual variants before committing to final art direction.

use_cases.title: Edit Reference Images
use_cases.description: Apply background swaps, headline changes, and identity-preserving adjustments using image-to-image routes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of [subject] in [setting]. Use a [style] look and save the result to my output folder.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Product Concept Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate four concept images for [product]. Keep the same lighting, vary composition, and use fast draft settings.

prompt_templates.title: Edit a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this image: [URL]. Keep [protected elements] unchanged, and change only [target element] to [new result].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Model-Routed Campaign Asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a final [campaign format] for [brand]. The text must read exactly [copy]. Choose the best RunComfy route and explain the choice.

output_examples.input: A designer asks for a poster with exact Japanese headline text.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects GPT Image 2, explains the typography reason, and generates a landscape poster through RunComfy.

output_examples.input: A product manager asks for ten quick coffee mug concepts.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses a fast draft route, creates batches, and keeps resolution low for iteration speed.

output_examples.input: A creator provides a portrait URL and asks for a new background.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses an image-edit route, preserves the subject, and changes only the requested scene.

best_practices: Use explicit preservation language for image edits.
Keep web grounding disabled unless the user requests real-world matching.
Start with draft settings, then increase quality for final assets.
anti_patterns: Do not paste RunComfy tokens into prompts or project files.
Do not use untrusted reference URLs without user approval.
Do not force one model for every visual task.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI to call RunComfy cloud model endpoints.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes image-to-image routes for reference-based edits and layout changes.

faq.question: Does it handle exact text in images?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends GPT Image 2 for precise typography and multilingual text.

faq.question: What data leaves my machine?
faq.answer: Prompts, settings, and referenced image URLs are sent to RunComfy services.

faq.question: Is a RunComfy token required?
faq.answer: Yes. Authentication is required through login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.
